title: jacobaea incana subsp. carniolica
intro: Jacobaea incana subsp. carniolica, also known as Hoary Cudweed, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hoary Cudweed include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Hoary Cudweed is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be prepared by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using a solvent like ethanol to extract its active compounds.
